food_subgroup: Poultry-based grain bowl meal with soup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a rotisserie chicken energy bowl meal. FOODON_00002473 (chicken dish) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (chicken meat, cooked) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for a composite grain bowl meal with rotisserie chicken and soup.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this composite meal type.

allergens: gluten (possible from grain base or soup)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a grain and vegetable energy bowl topped with rotisserie chicken and accompanied by a cup of soup, as typically served at Subway Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, roasted (USDA SR Legacy 05013) combined with composite grain bowl estimation from USDA FoodData Central mixed dish references
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Energy Bowl with Rotisserie Chicken and Soup' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated by component: rotisserie chicken (USDA SR Legacy 05013), mixed grain and vegetable base (USDA FoodData Central composite grain bowl references), and chicken broth soup (USDA SR Legacy 06172). Per-100g values represent a weighted blend of the full meal (approximately 40% chicken, 40% grain/vegetable base, 20% soup) normalised to 100 g. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and vegetable bowl with lean protein analogous estimate; chicken and vegetable dishes with grain base
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific meal. GI of 45 is estimated from analogous mixed grain bowl with lean protein entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Mixed grain and vegetable bowls with lean protein typically fall in the GI range 40–55 (low to medium). A conservative low-GI estimate of 45 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 52.4) / 100 ≈ 23.6, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 11.5 × 4.5 = 51.75 g ≈ 52.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full 450 g serving delivers approximately 1395 mg sodium, representing 58–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soup or dressing options and limit portion size to manage sodium intake from this meal, which can contribute substantially to daily sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated glycemic index of approximately 45 and moderate carbohydrate content of 11.5 g per 100 g; high protein content from rotisserie chicken supports glycaemic control.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This meal is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI, high protein content, and moderate carbohydrate load; however, portion size and soup sodium content should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat content (1.0 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium (310 mg per 100 g); full serving sodium load may be a concern for cardiovascular risk management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose this meal with low-sodium soup and avoid high-fat dressings, as the sodium content of a full serving may exceed recommended limits for heart health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Energy Bowl with Rotisserie Chicken and Soup is a Western-style meal set sold in Singapore through outlets such as Subway and mix-and-match food concepts. It consists of a grain and vegetable energy bowl topped with sliced or shredded rotisserie-roasted chicken, accompanied by a cup of warm soup, typically chicken broth or a light vegetable-based soup. The rotisserie chicken provides a savoury, lightly seasoned flavour with a tender, moist texture from the slow-roasting process. The grain base may include brown rice, quinoa, or mixed grains, contributing dietary fibre and complex carbohydrates. The meal is positioned as a higher-protein, lower-carbohydrate alternative to traditional sandwich meals. Nutritionally, the meal delivers approximately 118 kcal per 100 g, with a strong protein contribution of around 10.2 g per 100 g from the chicken component, moderate carbohydrates of 11.5 g per 100 g from the grain base, and relatively low fat at 3.8 g per 100 g. The estimated glycemic index of 45 places this meal in the low-GI category, making it broadly suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels. The sodium content is a clinical consideration, as the soup component can contribute significantly to total sodium intake. This meal is relevant for individuals seeking a balanced, protein-rich lunch or dinner option in a fast-casual setting in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific meal exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated by component weighting using USDA SR Legacy references for rotisserie chicken, grain bowl components, and chicken broth. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component references (rotisserie chicken USDA 05013, grain/vegetable base composite, chicken broth USDA 06172) weighted at approximately 40% chicken, 40% grain-vegetable base, 20% soup, normalised to 100 g. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the component source references used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the component source references used for this estimation. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the component source references used for this estimation. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed negligible/trace for lean roasted chicken and grain-based dishes per USDA SR Legacy data. 6. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific meal product is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. The food is sold at Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match outlets on field survey data at SGD 17.30. 8. Halal status is not confirmed for this specific meal set; Subway Singapore outlets may or may not be halal-certified depending on location. 9. The cuisine tag 'Sandwich' in the input record reflects the field survey platform category for Subway; the canonical cuisine classification is Western. 10. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (45 × 52.4) / 100 ≈ 23.6 g, based on 450 g serving with 11.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.
